title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Marine biologists in Cape Cod launched a GoFundMe to support North Atlantic right whale conservation efforts.
The fundraiser aims to gather $15,000 but has collected only $630 so far for research and specialized equipment.
North Atlantic right whales are critically endangered, with vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements as major threats.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots acquired Randy Moss from Raiders, resulting in an NFL record of 23 touchdowns in a single season.
Wes Welker was traded from Dolphins to Patriots in 2007, becoming a prolific slot receiver with no Super Bowl wins.
Brandin Cooks joined Patriots in 2017, achieving 1,082 yards and seven touchdowns, but was traded after one season.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected for arguing a check swing call during the Red Sox's 6-5 loss to the Cincinnati Reds.
Cora expressed frustration over umpire C.B. Bucknor not consulting the first base umpire on the check swing decision.
Trevor Story and Roman Anthony commented on the challenges and calls, with Anthony regretting an aggressive challenge decision.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story disputed a strike three call by umpire C.B. Bucknor, leading to manager Alex Cora's ejection.
Story was frustrated that Bucknor did not consult the first base umpire on the check swing decision.
The Red Sox tied the game in the ninth but lost 6-5 in 11 innings, with limited offensive support.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals in 16 games since returning from the Olympic break for the Boston Bruins.
A concussion prevented Zacha from participating in the 2026 Olympics with Czechia, motivating him for the NHL season.
Zacha has hit a career-high 26 goals this season and plays a crucial role in Boston's successful second line.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray's Red Sox debut ended in a no-decision as Boston lost 6-5 to the Cincinnati Reds.
Gray pitched four innings, allowing four runs, including a solo home run by Sal Stewart in the third.
Manager Alex Cora highlighted Gray's improvement after a rocky start, despite Boston never leading the game.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 gathered at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' rally, part of a nationwide protest initiative.
Speakers, including Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, addressed political threats and encouraged activism.
The event featured performances by Dropkick Murphys and action tables for signing up with advocacy groups.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at House of Blues, playing 17 songs including 'Pink Pony Club'.
The band offered a theatrical performance with fake blood, social satire, and brutal humor.
Highlights included character interactions with oversized effigies and a tribute to late member Dave Brockie.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are listed as questionable for Sunday's game against the Charlotte Hornets.
Brown is day-to-day with left Achilles tendinitis; Tatum has right Achilles management and may need more rest.
Nikola Vucevic is out with a right ring finger fracture, impacting the Celtics' upcoming lineup.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 rallies in Massachusetts.
Speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and Sen. Ed Markey, with performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Participants, including veterans and church groups, expressed concerns about the country's direction and the need for activism.
